snippets.title: 95% t-interval for a sample mean
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from scipy import stats

latencies_ms = np.array([112, 118, 125, 130, 115, 122, 119, 128, 121, 117])
n = len(latencies_ms)
mean = latencies_ms.mean()
se = latencies_ms.std(ddof=1) / np.sqrt(n)
t_crit = stats.t.ppf(0.975, df=n - 1)

lower = mean - t_crit * se
upper = mean + t_crit * se
print(f"mean={mean:.2f} ms, 95% CI=[{lower:.2f}, {upper:.2f}]")
snippets.explanation: Use the t distribution when σ is unknown and estimated by sample standard deviation s. `ddof=1` gives the unbiased s.

snippets.title: 95% Wilson interval for a proportion
snippets.code:
from statsmodels.stats.proportion import proportion_confint

successes, n = 85, 100
lower, upper = proportion_confint(successes, n, alpha=0.05, method="wilson")
print(f"p_hat={successes/n:.3f}, 95% Wilson CI=[{lower:.3f}, {upper:.3f}]")
snippets.explanation: Wilson intervals behave better than the basic Wald formula for small n or extreme p̂. Prefer Wilson or Clopper–Pearson in production reporting.

snippets.title: Bootstrap CI for a custom metric
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

rng = np.random.default_rng(0)
scores = rng.normal(0.82, 0.05, size=500)  # per-example correctness

def bootstrap_mean(data, n_boot=2000, alpha=0.05):
    boots = [rng.choice(data, size=len(data), replace=True).mean()
             for _ in range(n_boot)]
    lo, hi = np.quantile(boots, [alpha / 2, 1 - alpha / 2])
    return lo, hi

lo, hi = bootstrap_mean(scores)
print(f"mean={scores.mean():.3f}, 95% bootstrap CI=[{lo:.3f}, {hi:.3f}]")
snippets.explanation: When no closed-form SE exists (e.g. macro-F1 from grouped data), resampling approximates the sampling distribution and yields a percentile CI.
